citations.description: Ticket comment by Joe Oakhart: 'the precise per-run wall-clock/token figures on live traffic are captured by the drift_refresh path's own coverage/logging and roll up into the epic's deferred post-deploy measurement (72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d).' — measurement explicitly deferred to another ticket.

findings.criterion: The token/latency measurement (probe vs full review on a representative ticket) is recorded in a comment on this ticket.
findings.detail: The ticket comment from Joe Oakhart provides a structural criteria-count analysis (83% reduction in agent calls, 2 vs 12 criteria) but explicitly defers the actual per-run wall-clock/token measurement to the epic's post-deploy tracking ticket (72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d). The comment states: 'the precise per-run wall-clock/token figures on live traffic are captured by the drift_refresh path's own coverage/logging and roll up into the epic's deferred post-deploy measurement (72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d).' The criterion requires measuring against 'a representative ticket (an f1fa-shaped fixture: signed PASS, plan unchanged, HEAD advanced)' and recording those numbers here. The structural ratio is a proxy argument, not an actual probe-vs-full-review run measurement on such a fixture recorded in this ticket.
findings.remediation: Record in a ticket comment the actual token/latency numbers from a drift-refresh probe vs a full re-review on a representative fixture (signed PASS, plan unchanged, HEAD advanced), naming the specific run, model, wall-clock time, and LLM call counts observed.

citations.description: 'This is the third of three deterministic Pass-3 floors — novelty…, material freshness (drift-refresh, ADR 0002), and delivered-completion (this) — each along an independent axis and inert by default.' — undated, not updated.
Config table has no `verify.progressive_drift_refresh` row showing `true` default.
`verify.progressive_drift_refresh = false` shown with no 'Default ON' or back-out framing, unlike remediation_mode which shows `= true` with explicit 'Default ON' note.

findings.criterion: The stale-framing sweep leaves zero default-OFF claims about drift-refresh in src/, tests/, docs/ (dated ADR notes excepted).
findings.detail: Two residual default-OFF framings remain in docs/ outside dated ADR notes: (1) docs/plan-review-gate.md line 368 states 'each along an independent axis and inert by default' in reference to all three Pass-3 floors including drift-refresh — this is not in a dated note and was not updated. The config table in docs/plan-review-gate.md (lines 372–382) has no entry for `verify.progressive_drift_refresh` showing its new `true` default. (2) docs/config.md line 100 presents `verify.progressive_drift_refresh = false` (the back-out value) with only the bare description 'progressively refresh drifted findings during plan review' — no 'Default ON' framing — in contrast to `remediation_mode` at line 109 which shows `= true` with an explicit 'Default ON (operator-authorized 2026-07-11)' note. The ticket explicitly listed docs/plan-review-gate.md (the three-floors table) as a required update target.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
